title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 November 2024.

items.title: Gearbox, Bethesda and Epic veterans reveal their new multiplayer ‘suburban invasion’ game
bullets: Ruckus Games raises $19m in funding round led by Krafton. Bitkraft Ventures, Transcend, and Hypergryph also contributed to the round, among others.
Ruckus was founded in 2021 by Gearbox and Riot alumni, and is led by CEO Paul Sage, formerly creative director on Borderlands 3.
The currently unnamed project is described as a “session-based suburban invasion game combining RPG elements, crafting, town building and more”.

items.title: **Microsoft** reveals its new marketing campaign: ‘This is an Xbox’
bullets: Xbox's new slogal explains how its games can be played on numerous devices

items.title: **Valve** accused of "allowing the proliferation of hate" as report finds 1.8m instances of extremist or hateful content on Steam
bullets: ADL's Center on Extremism says it has discovered "millions of examples of extremist and hateful content"
The ADL highlighted that Valve's automated content moderation tools can be easily bypassed using altered words and ASCII art.

items.title: **Epic Games** is officially cool with the Internet Archive preserving early Unreal games
bullets: Epic Games has given the green light for the Internet Archive to host installation files for Unreal and its immediate follow-up, Unreal Tournament.

items.title: **Embracer** divests mobile studio Easybrain in $1.2 billion deal
bullets: Miniclip will become Easybrain's new owner when the deal concludes in early 2025
